response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the discount, and the customer will pay less for each item. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be reduced by 15%, which means the customer pays $200 minus 15% of $200, resulting in $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will be reduced by 15%, which means the customer pays $150 minus 15% of $150, resulting in $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items during the promotional week is $170 plus $127.50, which equals $297.50. ***

The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. This means that each item is reduced by 15% of its original price. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be reduced by 15%, resulting in $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will be reduced by 15%, resulting in $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items during the promotional week is $170 plus $127.50, which equals $297.50. ***

The customer pays $170 for the handcrafted table and $127.50 for the factory-made chairs during the promotional week. This results in a total payment of $297.50.
